question_text: Let 𝑋 be a single observation from a distribution having a probability density function 𝑓𝜃, 𝜃∈Θ. For testing 𝐻0 ∶𝜃= 1 against 𝐻1 ∶𝜃≠1, under which of the following options a uniformly most powerful test of level 0.05 exists?

options.A: 𝑓𝜃(𝑥) = { 𝜃 if 0 < 𝑥< 𝜃 0 otherwise ; Θ = (0, ∞)
options.B: 𝑓𝜃(𝑥) = {𝑒− (𝑥−𝜃) if 𝑥> 𝜃 0 otherwise ; Θ = (−∞, ∞)
options.C: 𝑓𝜃(𝑥) = { 𝜃𝑒− 𝑥 𝜃 if 𝑥> 0 0 otherwise ; Θ = (0, ∞)
options.D: 𝑓𝜃(𝑥) = { 𝜃𝑒− 𝑥−1 𝜃 if 𝑥> 1 0 otherwise ; Θ = (0, ∞)
